connection.fillRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields
connection.saveConnectionHint: Save a connection to quickly reconnect later

search.keywordsTooltip: Keyword / full-text search (BM25 or FTS). Enter text to combine with vector search for hybrid results.
search.noResults: No results found. The search vector may not match any documents in the collection.
search.noVectorFields: No vector fields available in collection
search.vectorFieldNotFound: Vector field "{{field}}" not found in collection schema
search.vectorFieldNotInSchema: Vector field not found in schema
search.sparseVectorDetected: Sparse vector format detected but selected field is not sparse
search.sparseVectorLengthMismatch: Sparse vector indices and values must have the same length
search.invalidSparseFormat: Invalid sparse vector format. Expected {indices: [], values: []}
search.noVectorInDocument: No vector found in this document
search.enterVectorOrKeywords: Enter a vector to search, or use keywords when the server supports hybrid search.

embedding.title: Generate Embedding
embedding.successMessage: Embedding generated! Vector copied to search field ({{dimension}}D)
embedding.howItWorksDesc: Embedding functions convert your text or files into vector embeddings that can be used for semantic search. You can create custom functions that connect to any embedding API or service.
embedding.workflowStep1: Select or create an embedding function (defines how to call your API)
embedding.workflowStep2: Choose input type: Text or File
embedding.workflowStep3: Enter your text or upload a file
embedding.workflowStep4: Click "Generate Embedding" to create the vector
embedding.workflowStep5: The generated vector is automatically copied to the search field below
embedding.privacyDesc: All embedding functions and API keys are stored locally on your computer. Your data never leaves your device when generating embeddings.

documents.bulkDeleteFilteredConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all documents matching the current filter?
documents.currentlyShowing: Currently showing {{count}} documents that may be affected
documents.deleteAllConfirm: You are about to delete ALL documents in this collection!

upsert.denseVectorError: Dense vector "{{field}}" must be an array of numbers
upsert.binaryVectorError: Binary vector "{{field}}" must be an array of numbers (0-255)
upsert.sparseVectorLengthError: Sparse vector "{{field}}": indices and values must have same length
upsert.sparseVectorNumericError: Sparse vector "{{field}}": indices and values must be numeric
upsert.vectorRequired: At least one vector is required

embeddingConfig.whatAreFunctionsDesc: Embedding functions allow you to generate vector embeddings from text or files using any API or service. They run in a secure sandbox and can integrate with OpenAI, Hugging Face, local models, or any custom endpoint.
embeddingConfig.name: Name
embeddingConfig.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this function does
embeddingConfig.functionSignatureDesc: Your function must be named embed and accept four parameters:
embeddingConfig.returnValueDesc: Return an array of numbers representing the embedding vector. The array length should match your collection's vector dimension.
embeddingConfig.securityPrivacyDesc: Functions run in a secure sandbox. They can make HTTP requests but cannot access the filesystem or Node.js APIs. All functions and API keys are saved locally on your computer only and never leave your device.
embeddingConfig.howToWrite: How to write an embedding function
embeddingConfig.codeShouldContainEmbed: Code should contain an async function named "embed"

filter.addFilterHint: Click a field above or add manually

update.restartToApply: Restart the app to apply the update

visualize.colorByDisabled: Disabled when cluster preview is shown
visualize.clusteringTooltip: Clustering is performed on original high-dimensional vectors, not the 2D projection. This provides semantically meaningful clusters.
visualize.epsTooltip: Maximum distance for points to be considered neighbors (cosine distance)
visualize.minPtsTooltip: Minimum points required to form a cluster
visualize.nearestNeighborsTooltip: Computed in original high-dimensional vector space, not 2D projection. Green lines show connections.
visualize.umapDescription: Preserves both local and global structure
visualize.tsneDescription: Great at revealing local cluster structure
visualize.pcaTooltip: Points that are close in the original space may appear far apart in this 2D projection because PCA reduces dimensions and loses information. The green lines show true neighbors from the vector database. Consider using UMAP or t-SNE for better cluster visualization.
visualize.umapTooltip: UMAP preserves neighborhood structure better than PCA. Points that are close in this 2D projection should also be close in the original high-dimensional space. Green lines show true neighbors from the vector database.
visualize.tsneTooltip: t-SNE excels at revealing local cluster structure. Points that are close together form tight, well-separated clusters. However, distances between clusters are not meaningful. Green lines show true neighbors from the vector database.
visualize.nNeighborsTooltip: Number of neighbors to consider. Higher values preserve more global structure, lower values preserve more local structure.
visualize.minDistTooltip: Minimum distance between points in the projection. Lower values create tighter clusters.
visualize.perplexityTooltip: Balance between local and global structure. Higher values consider more neighbors.
visualize.learningRateTooltip: Learning rate for optimization. Higher values may converge faster but be less stable.
visualize.iterationsTooltip: Number of optimization iterations. More iterations = better quality but slower.
visualize.noVectorsFound: No vectors found for the selected vector field
visualize.noVectorForDocument: No vector found for the selected document

main.selectCollection: Select a collection from the sidebar
